# Pre-releases

Tests for pre-release version handling in resolution.

## Basic pre-releases

### Pre-release with upper bound exclude

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::prerelease_upper_bound_exclude -->

Per PEP 440, pre-releases that match the upper bound should be excluded.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
flask<2.0.0
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --prerelease=allow
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile requirements.in --prerelease=allow
click==7.1.2
    # via flask
flask==1.1.4
    # via -r requirements.in
itsdangerous==1.1.0
    # via flask
jinja2==2.11.3
    # via flask
markupsafe==2.1.5
    # via jinja2
werkzeug==1.0.1
    # via flask

----- stderr -----
Resolved 6 packages in [TIME]
```

### Pre-release with upper bound include

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::prerelease_upper_bound_include -->

Per PEP 440, pre-releases should be included when the upper bound includes a pre-release version.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
flask<2.0.0rc4
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --prerelease=allow
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile requirements.in --prerelease=allow
click==8.1.7
    # via flask
flask==2.0.0rc2
    # via -r requirements.in
itsdangerous==2.1.2
    # via flask
jinja2==3.1.3
    # via flask
markupsafe==2.1.5
    # via
    #   jinja2
    #   werkzeug
werkzeug==3.0.1
    # via flask

----- stderr -----
Resolved 6 packages in [TIME]
```

### Pre-release alias

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::pre_alias -->

The `--pre` flag is an alias for `--prerelease=allow`.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
flask<2.0.0
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --pre
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile requirements.in --pre
click==7.1.2
    # via flask
flask==1.1.4
    # via -r requirements.in
itsdangerous==1.1.0
    # via flask
jinja2==2.11.3
    # via flask
markupsafe==2.1.5
    # via jinja2
werkzeug==1.0.1
    # via flask

----- stderr -----
Resolved 6 packages in [TIME]
```

### Pre-release in constraint

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::prerelease_constraint -->

A pre-release version in a constraint allows pre-releases for that package.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
flask
```

```toml constraints.txt
flask<=2.0.0rc2
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt
click==8.1.7
    # via flask
flask==2.0.0rc2
    # via
    #   -c constraints.txt
    #   -r requirements.in
itsdangerous==2.1.2
    # via flask
jinja2==3.1.3
    # via flask
markupsafe==2.1.5
    # via
    #   jinja2
    #   werkzeug
werkzeug==3.0.1
    # via flask

----- stderr -----
Resolved 6 packages in [TIME]
```

### Override with pre-release

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::requirement_override_prerelease -->

An override can restrict a pre-release requirement to stable versions.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
flask<2.0.0rc4
```

```toml overrides.txt
flask<2.0.1,!=2.0.0
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --override overrides.txt
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile requirements.in --override overrides.txt
click==7.1.2
    # via flask
flask==1.1.4
    # via
    #   --override overrides.txt
    #   -r requirements.in
itsdangerous==1.1.0
    # via flask
jinja2==2.11.3
    # via flask
markupsafe==2.1.5
    # via jinja2
werkzeug==1.0.1
    # via flask

----- stderr -----
Resolved 6 packages in [TIME]
```

## Pre-release preferences

### Existing pre-release preference

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::existing_prerelease_preference -->

When re-resolving, existing pre-release versions are preserved.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
cffi
```

```toml
# file: requirements.txt
cffi==1.17.0rc1
pyparser==2.22
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in -o requirements.txt --exclude-newer 2024-07-17T00:00:00Z
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile requirements.in -o requirements.txt
cffi==1.17.0rc1
    # via -r requirements.in
pycparser==2.22
    # via cffi

----- stderr -----
Resolved 2 packages in [TIME]
```

## Universal resolution with pre-releases

### Disjoint pre-release markers

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::universal_disjoint_prereleases -->

Different pre-release strategies with disjoint markers.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
cffi >= 1.16.0rc1 ; os_name != 'linux'
cffi >= 1.16.0rc1, <1.16.0rc2 ; os_name == 'linux'
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --universal --exclude-newer 2024-07-17T00:00:00Z
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile requirements.in --universal
cffi==1.16.0rc1
    # via -r requirements.in
pycparser==2.22
    # via cffi

----- stderr -----
Resolved 2 packages in [TIME]
```

### Disjoint pre-releases with allow flag

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::universal_disjoint_prereleases_allow -->

With `--prerelease=allow`, different versions are resolved for different forks.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
cffi >= 1.15.0, < 1.17.0 ; os_name == 'linux'
cffi >= 1.15.0, <= 1.16.0rc2 ; os_name != 'linux'
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --universal --prerelease allow --exclude-newer 2024-07-17T00:00:00Z
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile requirements.in --universal --prerelease allow
cffi==1.16.0rc2 ; os_name != 'linux'
    # via -r requirements.in
cffi==1.16.0 ; os_name == 'linux'
    # via -r requirements.in
pycparser==2.22
    # via cffi

----- stderr -----
Resolved 3 packages in [TIME]
```

### Transitive disjoint pre-release

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::universal_transitive_disjoint_prerelease_requirement -->

Different pre-release strategies for a package that's also a transitive dependency.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
cffi ; os_name == 'linux'
cffi >= 1.17.0rc1 ; os_name != 'linux'
cryptography
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --universal --exclude-newer 2024-07-17T00:00:00Z
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile requirements.in --universal
cffi==1.16.0 ; os_name == 'linux'
    # via
    #   -r requirements.in
    #   cryptography
cffi==1.17.0rc1 ; os_name != 'linux'
    # via
    #   -r requirements.in
    #   cryptography
cryptography==42.0.8
    # via -r requirements.in
pycparser==2.22
    # via cffi

----- stderr -----
Resolved 4 packages in [TIME]
```

### Global pre-release mode across forks

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::universal_prerelease_mode -->

Global pre-release mode is respected across all forks.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
cffi ; os_name == 'linux'
cffi >= 1.17.0rc1 ; os_name != 'linux'
```

```console
$ uv pip compile --prerelease=allow requirements.in --universal --exclude-newer 2024-07-17T00:00:00Z
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile --prerelease=allow requirements.in --universal
cffi==1.17.0rc1
    # via -r requirements.in
pycparser==2.22
    # via cffi

----- stderr -----
Resolved 2 packages in [TIME]
```
